propositions: Release actions are referenced by mutable major-version tags and branch-like refs, so two runs of the same source can execute different code; replace every uses: with a full 40-char commit SHA plus version comment.

Build tools install with unrestricted upgrades and no isolation control, so introduce a hash-locked constraints file installed via pip install --require-hashes --no-deps and build with python -m build --no-isolation to enforce Hatchling's version.

The MCP publisher is downloaded from a mutable releases/latest URL and executed without digest verification inside an id-token: write job, allowing a compromised archive to mint an OIDC token; split into an unprivileged mcp_verify job (download + sha256sum -c) and an OIDC-privileged mcp_registry job that consumes only the verified artifact.
Checkouts do not set persist-credentials: false, leaving the token in local git config; set persist-credentials: false on every release checkout.
upload-artifact drops the executable bit, so mcp_registry must chmod +x mcp-publisher after download-artifact or the publisher fails with Permission denied.

Add a PR-triggered release-lint workflow and a test-mcp-verify workflow to statically assert SHA pins, persist-credentials, --no-isolation, job permission structure, evidence fragment wiring, and corrupted-archive rejection, plus a docs/releasing.md Pinning section.
